tests.test_body:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-p1:16:16:16-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128"

define i10 @sub_to_and_nuw(i10 %x) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @sub_to_and_nuw(
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP1:%.*]] = and i10 [[X:%.*]], 120
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[R:%.*]] = add nuw nsw i10 [[TMP1]], 379
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i10 [[R]]
  %sub = sub nuw i10 71, %x
  %and = and i10 %sub, 120
  %r = sub i10 443, %and
  ret i10 %r
}

issue.author: chfast
issue.body:
I've found a case of `sub+and+sub` which can be combined into single `and`.

```llvm
define i64 @src(i64 %0) {
  %2 = sub nuw i64 71, %0
  %3 = and i64 %2, -8
  %4 = sub i64 64, %3
  ret i64 %4
}

define i64 @tgt(i64 %0) {
  %2 = and i64 %0, 120
  ret i64 %2
}
```
https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/MrCTxL

comments.author: RKSimon
comments.body: Any chance that you can generalize this further?

comments.author: chfast
bcl5980
comments.body:
It looks we can do it like:
https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/wq56Pq

But not sure it is most simple transform or not.
Candidate patch:
https://reviews.llvm.org/D136582
